hero.headline: Regenerative Sanctuary Portal
hero.tagline: Dwell with purpose. Live with consciousness.
hero.description: Join the community that chose Costa Rica as their sanctuary — living lands seeking conscious stewards.

home.section_description: Costa Rican sanctuaries under active protection, seeking conscious stewards.

technical_sheet.title: Technical Specification
technical_sheet.disclaimer: The information presented is based on public data and technical analysis. For specific legal terms, consult official documents.

flora_fauna.title: Living Sanctuary
flora_fauna.subtitle: Flora & Fauna
flora_fauna.description: A complete ecosystem where flora and fauna coexist in perfect harmony.

ancestral_wisdom.title: Ancestral Wisdom
ancestral_wisdom.subtitle: Indigenous Knowledge
ancestral_wisdom.description: Respect and integration of ancestral knowledge with modern conservation.
ancestral_wisdom.memory_boruca: Boruca Memory · Guardians of the South

division_pz.name: División Pérez Zeledón
division_pz.tagline: Where primary forest is the water bank the world needs.
division_pz.description: Nearly 600 hectares of primary and secondary forest within the La Amistad Pacific South Biological Corridor, designated as a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
division_pz.location: División, Pérez Zeledón, San José, Costa Rica
division_pz.technical_area: División, Pérez Zeledón (San José). 594 hectares of unified nature reserve.
division_pz.technical_water: Primary water source of the División River. Active commercial water concession.
division_pz.technical_zoning: Located within the Biosphere Reserve (UNESCO Certification). 91% forest coverage acting as a carbon sink.
division_pz.technical_elevation: 1,800 to 2,800 meters above sea level. Exceptional transition between humid tropical forest and montane cloud forest.
division_pz.technical_biodiversity: Pristine habitat with megafauna presence: pumas and tapirs (jaguars). Resplendent quetzal nesting zone.
division_pz.technical_potential: Limited construction potential to 5 hectares with maximum density of 1 dwelling per 10 hectares under sustainability restrictions.
division_pz.technical_privacy: Sole access via private road. Natural acoustic isolation due to mountainous topography.
division_pz.technical_logistics: Access via Route 237 (30 minutes to Uvita). Basic services: private spring water, installed solar energy, satellite communication.

la_carpintera.name: La Carpintera
la_carpintera.tagline: The perfect duality between strategic connectivity and ecological isolation.
la_carpintera.description: 380 hectares of cloud forest and transitional forest within the La Amistad Biological Corridor. Absolute sovereignty in water and energy.
la_carpintera.location: La Carpintera, San José, Costa Rica
la_carpintera.technical_area: La Carpintera (San José). 380 hectares of forest reserve with existing infrastructure.
la_carpintera.technical_water: 3 fresh water springs. Certified natural water capture and filtration system.
la_carpintera.technical_zoning: Buffer zone of the La Amistad Biosphere Reserve. Cloud forest in transition.
la_carpintera.technical_elevation: 1,600 to 2,200 meters above sea level. Unique microclimate with annual precipitation of 5,000mm.
la_carpintera.technical_biodiversity: Intact primary forest. Habitat for endemic species. Critical wildlife corridor.
la_carpintera.technical_potential: Limited development respecting forest coverage. Eco-tourism and research potential.
la_carpintera.technical_privacy: Natural isolation. Controlled access by terrain.
la_carpintera.technical_logistics: Strategic location with service access. Energy sovereignty with solar panels.

infrastructure.division_pz: Available services: Electricity, Internet, Telephone. Operational property.
infrastructure.la_carpintera: Available services: Water, Electricity, Internet. Existing infrastructure.

timeline.title: Boruca-Brunca Territory
timeline.description: The Boruca (Brunca) peoples establish their civilization in the División River basin. The land is understood as a living being with memory and sacred purpose.

timeline.title: Colonial Contact
timeline.description: Spanish conquest alters the territorial dynamics of the Southern Zone. The Boruca resist tenaciously and preserve much of their cosmovision and language to this day.

timeline.title: Agricultural Settlement
timeline.description: Colonial families establish themselves in Pérez Zeledón. Coffee, sugarcane, and cattle ranching begin to transform the landscape, while highland areas maintain their forest coverage.

timeline.title: Conservation Policies
timeline.description: Costa Rica establishes a pioneering conservation legal framework in Latin America. The La Amistad Biological Corridor is established, protecting the El Robledal territory.

timeline.title: UNESCO Recognition
timeline.description: The La Amistad Pacific South Conservation Area is confirmed as a World Heritage Site. El Robledal, within the corridor, acquires international conservation status.

timeline.title: Seeking Stewards
timeline.description: Nearly 600 hectares of primary forest seek a conscious steward. A life project that honors the World Heritage that this land represents.

timeline.title: Indigenous Territory
timeline.description: The region was ancestrally inhabited by Boruca, Bribri, and Cabécar peoples. These communities developed a profound relationship with the cloud forest, regarding it as sacred and essential to life.

timeline.title: Colonial Period
timeline.description: Spanish colonization reached the region, though the mountainous terrain allowed many indigenous communities to preserve their autonomy and ancestral knowledge.

timeline.title: Costa Rican Settlement
timeline.description: Costa Rican families settled in the lowlands. La Carpintera, in its height and isolation, was primarily utilized for sustainable agricultural activities.

timeline.title: Environmental Protection
timeline.description: Costa Rica recognizes the importance of cloud forests. The region enters protected areas, establishing limits on extractive development.

timeline.title: UNESCO Designation
timeline.description: La Carpintera formally integrates into the La Amistad Biosphere Reserve, recognized by UNESCO as a World Heritage Site.

timeline.title: Conscious Stewardship
timeline.description: La Carpintera seeks guardians to continue its conservation legacy. A space where ancestral tradition and modern sustainability converge.

prosperity.description: Strategic location within the UNESCO Biological Corridor, its own water volume and access from the Inter-American Highway make El Robledal a high-value asset. Its potential in ecotourism, organic production (active blackberries and avocado) and environmental services is immediate.

indicators.label: Ecotourism Potential
indicators.note: Direct access to biological corridors and active sustainable tourism certifications in the region.

indicators.label: Active Organic Production
indicators.note: Plots of cacao, banana and Boruca medicinal plants in production without agrochemicals.

indicators.label: Environmental Services Payment
indicators.note: Registered in FONAFIFO's Environmental Services Payment program — annual income guaranteed by forest conservation.

indicators.label: UNESCO Corridor Appreciation
indicators.note: Borders UNESCO Biosphere Reserve Core Zone — exceptional patrimonial appreciation.

indicators.label: Community Impact
indicators.note: Alliances with Boruca and Bribri communities for shared cultural and agricultural management.

issues.problem: Unemployment in Pérez Zeledón rural zones
issues.solution: Generation of green jobs: nature guides, organic farmers, local artisans integrated into the project.
Active biological corridors and reforestation with native species that increase World Heritage value.
Model of responsible water stewardship: private springs supply neighboring communities under collaboration agreement.

ancestral.title: Boruca Memory — Guardians of the South
ancestral.description: The Boruca (Brunca) nation has inhabited these lands for millennia. Their worldview recognizes in every river, mountain and tree a living being with memory and purpose. The Festival of the Little Devils, celebrated annually near these lands, is the oldest cultural resistance in Central America.

lifestyle: Awake with cloud forest chorus — transition of life zones between 1,800 and 2,800 msnm
Eternal spring climate: 15 to 22°C year-round without mechanical air conditioning
Hikes on 7 km of internal trails in primary forest — direct access to División River springs
Certified water from private spring, captured from mountain water torrents
At 2 km from División community · 30 km (45 min) from San Gerardo de Dota · 35 km (45 min) from San Isidro
Starlit skies without light pollution in one of Central America's cleanest basins (UNESCO)
Operational sovereignty: direct access from paved Inter-American Route + complete service infrastructure

potencial.title: Integrated Wellness
potencial.desc: El Robledal offers a superlative scenario for comprehensive health. Spanning elevations from 1,800 to 2,800 msnm, it guarantees perpetual comfort climate. Its high-mountain air and complete water sovereignty (owning the very source of División River) create a pristine environment, ideal for establishing isolated sanctuaries.

potencial.title: Environmental Conservation
potencial.desc: Mega-asset of conservation with 594 hectares and 91% forest cover, functioning as critical anchor for Biological Corridor connectivity toward the Central Pacific. Its location within UNESCO Biosphere Reserve territory and presence of megafauna like tapirs and pumas grant it unmatched international validation apart from its important water resources.

potencial.title: Education and Research
potencial.desc: Houses within a single property the transition of all Holdridge Life Zones, converting it into a real-scale ecological laboratory. It is the ideal location to establish an international university station focused on watershed management and climate change.

potencial.title: Regenerative Tourism
potencial.desc: Unlike other reserves with total restriction, El Robledal's land use allows low-density construction (dwellings every 10,000 m²). Leveraging its 7 km of internal roads and paved access, the buyer has the perfect canvas to develop a sustainable luxury eco-lodge.

potencial.title: Scientific Research
potencial.desc: The estate is the cradle of División River and key tributary of Savegre River. Monitoring tapir, feline and bird populations across these 600 hectares represents an inexhaustible opportunity to attract grants and high-level scientific tourism.

potencial.title: Spiritual Development
potencial.desc: The isolation provided by nearly 600 hectares, combined with the constant energy of pure water torrents, generates the atmosphere of silence and absolute isolation demanded by the world's most exclusive retreat projects.

potencial.title: Community Integration
potencial.desc: Establishing an institutional project here will mitigate migration and local poverty by halting deforestation and poaching through generation of stable green jobs (forest guardians, guides, hospitality).

descriptions.amor: Protecting 594 hectares of forest is the greatest act of love and commitment toward our planet. This sanctuary, backed by UNESCO certification, safeguards in perpetuity the pristine habitat of quetzals, pumas and tapirs, guaranteeing that the mountain's heartbeat is preserved intact for future generations.
descriptions.salud: An environment sculpted by nature for physical restoration and mental clarity. The unbreakable purity of cloud forest air, an eternal spring climate (15°C - 22°C) and absolute control over a private crystalline water spring offer perfect conditions for an absolute wellness lifestyle and healing.
descriptions.sabiduria: This region, guarded for millennia by the indigenous Boruca nation, possesses profound spiritual and historical dimension. Here, true wisdom is achieved by immersing oneself in the peace of its multiple ecosystems, learning from primary forest resilience and reconnecting with the sacred and silent purpose of the land.
descriptions.prosperidad: True modern wealth resides in autonomy and natural capital safeguarding. With direct access, operational infrastructure and confirmed legal viability for minimum-impact development, the property represents an incomparable trophy asset that guarantees peace, radical privacy and long-term security.

legacy.title: An Intergenerational Legacy
legacy.message: Inherited from grandfather and consolidated by the Ortiz brothers, El Robledal was purchased wholly to protect the integrity of 594 hectares. The sons of current owners have other life projects and wish to pass stewardship to an organization, NGO, foundation or individual who understands the macro-ecological value of this mega-reserve. Whoever receives it becomes guardian of División River's spring, custodian of a World Heritage Site, protector of native megafauna and guarantor of pure water for future generations. This is the call of the La Amistad Pacífico Sur Biological Corridor.

quetzal_pz.name: Resplendent Quetzal
quetzal_pz.description: Sacred bird of Mesoamerica. Documented at El Robledal as permanent breeder in the highest elevation zones (2,000+ msnm). Its presence confirms forest maturity with abundant wild avocado.

tapir_danta.name: Baird's Tapir
tapir_danta.description: Megafauna characteristic of the La Amistad Biological Corridor. El Robledal offers optimal habitat as seed disperser in high-elevation primary forest zones.
tapir_danta.habitat: Primary and secondary tropical forest, 1,200–3,000 msnm

coyote.name: Coyote
coyote.description: Key predator of El Robledal ecosystem. Present in primary forest zones as regulator of small mammal populations.

halcon_rojo.name: Red-tailed Hawk
halcon_rojo.description: Native raptor of the Biological Corridor. Documented at El Robledal as indicator of pristine ecosystem. Patrols open forest zones searching for small prey.

venado_cola_blanca.name: White-tailed Deer
venado_cola_blanca.description: Herbivorous megafauna documented at El Robledal. Present in primary forest zones as indicator of low hunting pressure ecosystem. Key seed disperser.

puma.name: Puma (Mountain Lion)
puma.description: Apex predator of the La Amistad Biological Corridor. Documented at El Robledal as critical ecological regulator. Its presence indicates ecosystem in excellent conservation state.
puma.habitat: Primary and secondary tropical forest, 1,200–3,000 msnm

zorro.name: Gray Fox
zorro.description: Medium-sized carnivore of El Robledal ecosystem. Predator of small mammals and fruits contributing to primary forest ecological balance.

perezoso_dos_dedos.name: Hoffmann's Two-toed Sloth
perezoso_dos_dedos.description: Arboreal mammal specialized in El Robledal's forest cover. Its presence in primary forest zones indicates ecosystem stability and maturity.

conejo.name: Central American Rabbit
conejo.description: Small herbivorous mammal of the Biological Corridor. Documented at El Robledal as key food source for medium predators and raptors.

liebre.name: Black-tailed Jackrabbit
liebre.description: Hopping mammal present in open forest zones. Indicator of forest cover mosaic areas at El Robledal.

murcielago.name: Jamaican Fruit-eating Bat
murcielago.description: Crucial seed-dispersing chiropteran at El Robledal. Its nocturnal activity facilitates natural regeneration of primary forest.

zariguey.name: Virginia Opossum
zariguey.description: Omnivorous marsupial of El Robledal ecosystem. Present in primary forest zones as predator of invertebrates and fruits.

trogon.name: Violaceous Trogon
trogon.description: Colorful bird documented at El Robledal. Inhabits mature cloud forest zones in the property's highest elevations.

passiflora.name: Gilbertian Passionflower
passiflora.description: Costa Rican endemic species documented at El Robledal. One of 71 endemic plant species identified in the Savegre Biosphere Reserve. High conservation value plant.

aguacatillo.name: Aguacatillo
aguacatillo.description: Characteristic canopy tree of El Robledal's cloud forest. Preferred food of the Resplendent Quetzal. Its abundance on the property ensures permanent nesting of sacred birds.

roble.name: White Oak
roble.description: Emblematic tree that gives name to El Robledal. Master species of montane forest. Its presence indicates mature and stable primary forest in the highest elevation zones.

helecho_arborescente.name: Tree Fern
helecho_arborescente.description: Understory structurer of El Robledal's cloud forest. Creates crucial humid microhabitats for invertebrate fauna and amphibians. Indicator of extreme humidity and pristine conservation.

orquidea.name: Cloud Forest Orchids
orquidea.description: Family of epiphytic plants adorning the cloud forest canopy. Hundreds of species documented in the Biological Corridor. Their presence indicates high humidity and exceptional forest conservation.

musgo_liquen.name: Mosses and Lichens
musgo_liquen.description: Key components of aerial biomass at El Robledal. Absorb moisture and nutrients from perpetual mist. Indicators of conservation state and mountain air purity.

mora.name: Blackberry
mora.description: Productive fruit shrub cultivated in delimited zones at El Robledal. Wildlife food and agroforestry product. Coexists with strict primary forest conservation.

aguacate.name: Avocado
aguacate.description: Agroforestry crop integrated in productive zones at El Robledal. Maintained under sustainability principles. Complements primary forest conservation at 594-hectare scale.

prosperity.description: Absolute ecological protection with 10 years of active stewardship that eliminated illegal hunting and logging. With 85-90% forest density, it functions as a carbon sink and biological refuge free of agrochemicals. Active beekeeping, fauna ponds and 100% solar operation demonstrate that inhabiting the ecosystem protects it.

indicators.label: Forest Cover
indicators.note: 85-90% dense montane cloud forest (85-90% pure forest). Active carbon sink and biological corridor of maximum density.

indicators.label: Environmental Purity
indicators.note: 12 years free of agrochemicals. Active beekeeping, certified organic nursery and native fruit trees as biological indicators of exceptional quality.

indicators.label: Turnkey
indicators.note: Operational house, solar panels, Starlink, fauna ponds and beekeeping installed. Functional water system by gravity from spring.

indicators.label: No Legal Encumbrances
indicators.note: Without mortgages or liens. Active Property Tax exemption. Eligible for FONAFIFO Environmental Services Payment.

indicators.label: Operations Hub
indicators.note: Due to its compact size and high forest density, it functions as the perfect administrative headquarters for a larger conservation project.

issues.solution: Aqueduct easement legally bound to Estate 1 concession. Supply guaranteed by legal instrument independent of owner.
Specific technical replacement of battery bank. Opportunity to modernize system to latest generation with immediate return.
House operable from day one. Preventive maintenance and adjustment of interior spaces to buyer's technical requirements (laboratories or offices).

ancestral.title: Huetar Memory and Coffee Heritage
ancestral.description: Huetar heritage transformed from coffee plantation and dairy to voluntary forest restoration free of agrochemicals for over a decade, healing soils and recovering the water cycle. — HUETAR MEMORY · LA CARPINTERA

potencial.title: Integrated Wellness
potencial.desc: The properties in the La Carpintera Hills offer an unmatched refuge for comprehensive health. Their elevation provides a cool microclimate, pure cloud forest air and a natural barrier against pollution, creating a pristine and secure environment minutes from the city; the ideal setting to establish wellness sanctuaries founded on the pillar of health.

potencial.title: Environmental Conservation
potencial.desc: This mega-asset acts as a fundamental bastion for the La Carpintera Hills Protected Zone. Its forest cover guarantees essential biological connectivity of the Central Valley, protecting vital water sources and serving as a safe refuge for endemic species, giving it incalculable conservation value.

potencial.title: Education and Research
potencial.desc: Its geographic position as a natural climate filter in the valley converts it into a living ecological laboratory. It is the ideal location to establish an academic station or research center focused on cloud forest resilience and urban ecology, actively fostering the pillar of wisdom.

potencial.title: Regenerative Tourism
potencial.desc: Its strategic value lies in unmatched accessibility combined with absolute natural isolation. Land use and topography provide the perfect canvas for developing a low-density eco-lodge or volunteer programs where prosperity is achieved through a sustainable and highly exclusive model.

potencial.title: Scientific Research
potencial.desc: The richness of its ecosystems and its function as a green lung represent an inexhaustible opportunity. Monitoring the flora, birdlife and mammals of this biological corridor is a key attraction for forging academic alliances, securing international funding (grants) and promoting high-level scientific tourism.

potencial.title: Spiritual Development
potencial.desc: The atmosphere of the cloud forest, wrapped in mist and profound silence, combined with the high topographic security of the estates, generates an energy of absolute isolation. It is the perfect atmosphere of peace demanded by the world's most exclusive retreat projects, directly cultivating the pillar of love and inner connection.

potencial.title: Community Integration
potencial.desc: Developing an institutional and integrated project on these estates will mitigate urban pressure on the mountain, stopping deforestation by generating stable green jobs (forest guardians, nature guides, hospitality) and spreading prosperity and environmental education to surrounding communities.

legacy.title: A Platform Ready for the Next Cycle
legacy.message: This property transformed from coffee plantation and goat dairy to dense cloud forest thanks to 12 years of active stewardship and natural regeneration without chemical intervention. Today it is the purest conservation asset in the corridor. The owner offers complete technical accompaniment during transition, with deep knowledge of the water ecosystem, biodiversity and installed energy sovereignty infrastructure. This estate awaits someone who understands that conserving is the most intelligent way to invest.

quetzal_carpintera.name: Resplendent Quetzal
quetzal_carpintera.description: Sacred bird of Mesoamerica. Indicator of mature forest. Present seasonally in La Carpintera between 1,550–1,800 msnm.

jilguero.name: Black-capped Nightingale-Thrush
jilguero.description: Endemic species of Costa Rica's highlands. Nocturnal cantor of the cloud forest canopy. Documented in La Carpintera as permanent resident.

roble_carpintera.name: White Oak
roble_carpintera.description: Majestic tree of great stature, characterized by robust trunk and dense crown. Key species of montane forest providing food and shelter to countless species. Its wood holds great cultural value in Costa Rica.

cedro.name: Spanish Cedar
cedro.description: Fast-growing tree with aromatic and high-value wood. Historic in Costa Rica for its cultural and economic importance. Restorer species that regenerates rapidly after disturbances.
cedro.habitat: Humid and very humid montane forest, 800–2,200 msnm

higueron.name: Strangler Fig
higueron.description: Fig tree with expansive crowns that harbor complete fauna ecosystems. Fruit producer year-round, essential food for birds, mammals and reptiles. Very important species in biological corridors.
higueron.habitat: Tropical and humid montane forest, 200–2,400 msnm

vaquita.name: Vaquita Orchid
vaquita.description: Epiphytic orchid with pendant inflorescences and aromatic flowers. Species dependent on stable moisture and temperature conditions of montane forest. Symbol of cloud forest ecological health.
vaquita.habitat: Lower and middle montane cloud forest, 1,400–2,200 msnm

lagartillo.name: Lagartillo
lagartillo.description: Very common forest species in Costa Rica, belonging to the Rutaceae family (same as citrus). Characterized by its bark covered in large conical spines and very resistant wood. Aromatic tree of small to medium stature with compound leaves and capsular fruits. Ancestrally used in traditional Costa Rican medicine. Food source for specialized wildlife.
lagartillo.habitat: Humid and deciduous montane forest, 1,000–2,100 msnm

helecho_carpintera.name: Tree Fern
helecho_carpintera.description: Ancient tree fern whose lineage traces back to past geological eras. Creates specialized ecosystems around its fibrous trunk. Critical indicator of high-altitude cloud forests without human intervention.
helecho_carpintera.habitat: Very humid montane cloud forest, 1,800–3,000 msnm
